claim_boundary: Read+review reconciliation of the electrical block-netlist + BOM against the mechanical CAD assembly-manifest (123 parts). It is not routed copper, not ERC/DRC, not a fabricated flex drawing, not a built unit. ACCOUNTED means the interconnect appears in the electrical plan, has a BOM line, and has a mechanical representation (part or modeled keepout/connector); it does NOT mean release-ready.

register.interconnect: Display MIPI-DSI + touch I2C + backlight FPC (40-pin)
register.mate: J_DISPLAY_TOUCH (block-netlist) - 10 DSI HS nets + DISP_BL_EN/PWM + TOUCH_I2C + DISP_AVDD_5V5/AVEE_N5V5
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom display_touch (40pin display FPC); bom-unit-cost fpcs_internal_interconnect 'Display FPC'
register.gap: Connector body modeled but pin count not parameterized in CAD; touch controller MPN still unspecified (display review blocker). Bend path modeled (bend_keepout, r=1.0mm).

register.interconnect: Rear camera MIPI-CSI FPC + connector (4-lane, 24-30 pin)
register.cad_part: rear_camera_module ONLY [16..26, 52.8..62.8, -4.3..0.8]; NO discrete camera FPC tail or connector part
register.mate: J_CAM0 (block-netlist) - CAM0 4 CSI lanes + I2C + AF I2C + reset/pwdn
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom rear_camera (24-30pin camera FPC conn); bom-unit-cost fpcs_internal_interconnect 'camera FPC x2'
register.gap: Electrical mate + BOM present, but CAD models only the camera module body. No camera-FPC tail, no board-side CSI connector, and no routing/bend keepout part in the manifest. Mechanical FPC route from rear-camera (top-right) to SoC connector is unmodeled.

register.mate: J_CAM1 (block-netlist) - CAM1 2 CSI lanes + I2C + reset
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom front_camera (small camera FPC conn); bom-unit-cost 'camera FPC x2'
register.gap: Same as rear: no front-camera FPC tail or board connector modeled in CAD; module body only. Module front face at Z=+2.6 sits above main_pcb top (Z=-1.7) - flex must reach down to the board, route unmodeled.

register.interconnect: Battery FPC/wires to charger + NTC thermistor + PCM
register.cad_part: battery_pouch ONLY; NO battery connector (J_BATTERY) part, NO wire/FPC, NO board-edge connector
register.mate: J_BATTERY (block-netlist) VBAT/GND + BAT_NTC + BAT_ID -> U_CHARGER CHG_TS
register.bom_line: bom-unit-cost battery_fpc ('connector/wire harness integrated in pack price'); battery_protection_pcm ('bundled with pack')
register.gap: Electrical + BOM present (4-pin pack: VBAT/GND/NTC/ID). CAD has no mating board connector part and no harness/FPC geometry. preliminary-bom flags pcb_dependency '4pin battery connector or soldered pack' unresolved. NTC line and PCM are electrically named but have no mechanical connector seat in the manifest.

register.type: side-key flex OR board-edge SMT switch
register.cad_part: power_button_cap, volume_button_cap (caps + gaskets + labyrinth rails ONLY); NO switch SMT body, NO side-key flex part
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom side_buttons qty3 XKB TS-1187A-B-A-B; bom-unit-cost fpcs_internal_interconnect 'side-button FPC'
register.gap: Caps + seals modeled; the actual 3x SMD tact switch bodies and the side-key FPC/flex (or left-edge switch island) are NOT in the CAD manifest. pcb_dependency 'side_key_flex_or_left_edge_switch_island' unresolved (buttons sit in the full-width battery zone per topology plan; spine cannot stay in battery zone). Volume rocker may foul SIM tray (io-buttons review open issue).

register.bom_line: preliminary-bom usb_c_receptacle GCT USB4105; bom-unit-cost line present
register.gap: Receptacle, reinforcement saddle, aperture, drip-lip, drain shelf, 4 gaskets all modeled. USB-C is on the bottom island so its USB2 pair must traverse the split flex (see top-bottom interconnect); USB2 SI across flex is an open electrical blocker but the connector itself is fully accounted.

register.type: hybrid power/signal FPC bridge (Hirose BM28 class) OR board-to-board
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom top_bottom_interconnect Hirose BM28; bom-unit-cost fpcs_internal_interconnect
register.gap: Both connector bodies + flex routing modeled in CAD AND electrically allocated (49 contacts, 25 active nets, 18 GND, 6 spare). BUT exact mating MPN, circuit count, flex stackup, USB2 SI, and current rise are all release blockers; schematic is a text scaffold, footprints are placeholders. CONTRADICTS single main_pcb (see reconciliation #2).

register.cad_part: cellular_top_antenna_keepout + cellular_bottom_antenna_keepout (KEEPOUT VOLUMES ONLY); NO feed clip/spring/coax/feed-pad part
register.bom_line: bom-unit-cost antennas 'Cellular main + diversity FPC ... laser-cut copper-on-PI'
register.gap: BOM names the antenna set; CAD provides only reserved keepout volumes. No feed-point part (spring contact, pogo, coax, or laser-direct feed pad) modeled connecting the antenna element to the RF front end. Feed transition is unmodeled mechanically.

register.cad_part: shares cellular_top/bottom_antenna_keepout; NO dedicated diversity feed part
register.gap: Diversity element location not distinctly modeled; only generic antenna keepouts exist. No feed point. Two cellular keepouts (top+bottom) exist but are not labeled main vs diversity.
Keepout reserved; no feed clip/pad modeled. Two RF ports (RF0/RF1) electrically but single keepout volume.

register.cad_part: NONE - no GNSS-specific keepout or feed part in manifest
register.bom_line: not separately lined; folded into cellular antenna set / cellular module
register.gap: block-netlist routes CELL_GNSS_RF from the modem but there is NO GNSS antenna element, keepout, or feed in the CAD manifest and no distinct BOM antenna line. GNSS either shares the cellular element (not stated/modeled) or is unaccounted. Honest gap.

register.interconnect: Antenna aperture tuner (Qorvo QPC1252Q) RFFE + antenna connection
register.type: SMT IC + RFFE control bus + RF series element
register.mate: RFFE bus from U_CELL (RG255C modem); placement in cellular_bottom antenna feed region per params
register.gap: Tuner IC modeled in CAD, BOM-lined, placed in the cellular_bottom feed region. RFFE control is modem-programmed (params). Tuner-to-antenna-element RF link relies on the same unmodeled feed-point gap as the cellular antenna, but the tuner itself is accounted.

register.type: spring contacts or solder pads to bottom-island audio amp
register.cad_part: bottom_speaker_module + acoustic_chamber; NO discrete contact/pad/flex part
register.gap: Module on bottom island near the amp; spring/solder contacts not separately modeled but are integral to the module per audio review (commodity 1115 box). Accepted as accounted; contact method (spring vs solder tab) should be frozen with vendor drawing.

Receiver sits at top of phone (Y~+69) but the audio codec/amp is on the BOTTOM island (Y<0). No earpiece flex/spring routing the ~140mm to the top is modeled. Electrically the earpiece is not even broken out as a distinct connector in block-netlist (only SPK_P/N for the loud speaker). Top-mounted earpiece-to-bottom-codec route is an unmodeled gap.

Bottom mic is near the bottom audio island - on-board, OK. TOP mic (Y~+? top edge) needs a PDM flex to the bottom codec; no top-mic flex modeled. PDM bus crosses the split flex (it is in the split-interconnect audio_control nets) but top-mic-to-board flex is unmodeled.

register.bom_line: bundled in audio subsystem; HAPTIC_OUT crosses split flex (haptic_test net)
register.gap: LRA modeled on bottom-right; HAPTIC_OUT is allocated on the split flex, but the LRA-to-driver lead (wires or FPC) is not a discrete CAD part. Driver IC location (top vs bottom island) not pinned.

register.interconnect: SIM / eSIM (tray contacts or eSIM solder)
register.type: nano-SIM tray w/ contacts + eSIM solder
register.bom_line: preliminary-bom sim_tray; bom-unit-cost sim_tray (nano-SIM tray + gasket)
register.gap: Tray outline + keepout modeled (right edge, -X... actually +X side X~+38). SIM contact/holder SMT part and the contact-to-modem flex are not discrete CAD parts. eSIM (if used) solder site not modeled. Tray on side edge may conflict with volume rocker (io-buttons review open issue).

register.type: drive lines from flash driver IC
register.mate: I2C flash driver AW36515 (block-netlist NFC/sensor I2C class; driver not its own block)
register.gap: LED modeled, window/baffle/septum modeled, LED + driver IC both BOM-lined. Driver is not a distinct block in block-netlist (rides the I2C bus) but is electrically implied and BOM-present. 1.0x1.0 seat now matches a 1.0x1.0 emitter (optical review fix applied).

register.cad_part: NONE - CAD models a bare-SoC mainboard (main_pcb single 64x132x0.8 + soc_shield_can 18x16mm). NO SODIMM socket, NO SoM daughterboard, NO stacked-height allocation
register.mate: U_SOC modeled as bare AP in block-netlist (full LPDDR_*/UFS_*/JTAG fanout) - matches bare-SoC, NOT a SoM edge connector
register.bom_line: bom-unit-cost PATH A = Firefly Core-3566JD4 RK3566 SoM, 260-pin SODIMM 0.5mm (default cost path)
register.gap: HARD CONTRADICTION. BOM default (PATH A) is a 260-pin SODIMM SoM daughterboard, but the mechanical CAD and the block-netlist both model a bare-SoC mainboard (discrete LPDDR/UFS/PMIC fanout, soc_shield_can, single 0.8mm board). No SODIMM socket part, and no stacked Z-height (a SODIMM SoM + socket adds ~3-4mm of stack the 11.8mm flush-back enclosure has not budgeted). The mechanical design is a chip-down (PATH B) model; the BOM ships PATH A. Must be reconciled - see reconciliation #1.

som_vs_bare_soc.mechanical_assumes: bare-SoC chip-down mainboard (PATH B): single main_pcb 64x132x0.8mm, soc_shield_can 18x16mm over the SoC, block-netlist exposes full LPDDR4/UFS/JTAG/boot fanout consistent with a bare AP placed directly on the board.
som_vs_bare_soc.bom_ships: PATH A turnkey RK3566 SoM (Firefly Core-3566JD4), 260-pin gold-finger SODIMM 0.5mm pitch daughterboard; lines for discrete LPDDR/eMMC/PMIC are zeroed/folded-into-SoM.
som_vs_bare_soc.physical_fit: A SODIMM SoM does NOT fit the current model as-is. A 260-pin SODIMM module + edge socket adds a stacked daughterboard (~1.0mm board + components + socket standoff, ~3-4mm Z) on top of the 0.8mm mainboard. The 11.8mm flush-back enclosure budget (battery 5.6 + swell 0.6 + display stack + 0.8 board) has no room for a stacked SoM over the mainboard; the soc_shield_can footprint (18x16) is far smaller than a SODIMM module (~67-70mm finger edge). The SoM is mechanically UNMODELED and UNBUDGETED.
som_vs_bare_soc.recommended_resolution: State honestly: the mechanical CAD targets a bare-SoC (chip-down) mainboard. The SoM (PATH A) is the buildable-now / bring-up electrical path (no NDA), but it does NOT fit the current flush-back mechanical model. Production target = chip-down bare RK3566 (PATH B, requires NDA ball-map). Either (a) declare the mechanical model PATH-B-only and tag PATH A as breadboard/EVT-bench-only (not in-enclosure), or (b) re-spec the enclosure for a SODIMM SoM stack (thicker, or horizontal SoM with its own keepout) and add the SODIMM socket as a CAD part + BOM-consistent stack. Current state = inconsistent.

single_vs_split_board.cad_models: ONE board: main_pcb is a single rectangle [-32..32, -66..66] = 64x132mm. PLUS split_interconnect_top_connector (Y+36..+38.7), split_interconnect_bottom_connector (Y-53.7..-51.3), and split_interconnect_side_flex spanning Y -51.5..+36.5 along the +X edge.
single_vs_split_board.electrical_plan_selects: TWO rigid islands: board-topology-decision selects top_bottom_rigid_islands_with_flex_or_board_to_board; block-netlist has J_TOP_BOTTOM_FLEX_TOP and _BOTTOM as the two-island bridge (49 contacts).
single_vs_split_board.contradiction: The CAD has BOTH a single continuous 64x132 main_pcb AND a top/bottom split-interconnect flex set. A single board does not need a top-to-bottom flex. The main_pcb spans Y -66..+66 continuously, yet the split connectors sit at Y+37 and Y-52 with a flex bridging them - implying the board should be cut into two islands around the center battery, which the topology decision explicitly calls for ('replace center-window board with top and bottom islands').
single_vs_split_board.recommended_resolution: The intended topology is TWO rigid islands joined by the 49-contact flex (battery occupies the full-width center). The mechanical main_pcb is a stale single-rectangle placeholder that has NOT been cut to match the selected split topology (board-topology-decision notes 'CAD replaced center-window board with top and bottom islands' but the manifest still shows one continuous main_pcb). Fix: split main_pcb into top_island (~Y+18..+66) and bottom_island (~Y-66..-46) parts in CAD to match the electrical plan and the split-interconnect flex that is already modeled. Until then the board geometry contradicts the interconnect.

top_3_missing_partial: MISSING: RK3566 SoM 260-pin SODIMM connector + daughterboard - BOM default ships a SoM, but CAD+netlist model a bare-SoC chip-down board with no socket, no daughterboard, and no stacked-Z budget. Largest discrepancy.
MISSING: GNSS antenna feed - CELL_GNSS_RF is routed from the modem in the netlist but there is no GNSS antenna element, keepout, or feed point in the CAD manifest and no distinct BOM antenna line.

PARTIAL: All antenna FEED POINTS (cellular main+diversity, Wi-Fi/BT) and ALL discrete component-to-board flexes (rear/front camera FPC, battery harness+connector, side-key flex, top-mic PDM flex, earpiece lead, SIM contact flex) are BOM-named but NOT modeled as CAD parts - only keepout volumes / module bodies exist. The mechanical FPC/feed routing layer is largely absent from the manifest.
